content.problemStatement:
Salmon runs a fish delivery company in Fish City. Fish City has NNN vertical roads numbered 1,2,...,N1, 2, ..., N1,2,...,N from left to right, each spaced 1 unit apart; similarly, there are MMM horizontal roads numbered 1,2,...,M1, 2, ..., M1,2,...,M from bottom to top, each spaced 1 unit apart. We will denote the intersection between the xxx-th vertical road and the yyy-th horizontal road as (x,y)(x, y)(x,y).

Salmon has to make KKK deliveries, with the iii-th delivery starting at (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i)(xiвЂ‹,yiвЂ‹) and ending at (xiвЂІ,yiвЂІ)(x'_i, y'_i)(xiвЂІвЂ‹,yiвЂІвЂ‹). He can also travel every road with speed 0.50.50.5 (meaning it would take him 222 units of time to travel 111 unit of distance). However, Salmon can bribe the mayor of Fish City and make the mayor change **exactly one horizontal** road to a highway, meaning the speed on that road would increase to 111.

He wants to choose a road to be a highway such that the sum of durations of all the deliveries is minimum possible. Help him find this minimum sum!

Note that each delivery starts exactly at (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i)(xiвЂ‹,yiвЂ‹) and ends at (xiвЂІ,yiвЂІ)(x'_i, y'_i)(xiвЂІвЂ‹,yiвЂІвЂ‹), i.e., we don't count the time taken to travel from (xiв€’1вЂІ,yiв€’1вЂІ)(x'_{i-1}, y'_{i-1})(xiв€’1вЂІвЂ‹,yiв€’1вЂІвЂ‹) to (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i)(xiвЂ‹,yiвЂ‹) in our answer.

###Input:
The first line contains 3 integers, NNN, MMM and KKK (1в‰¤N,M,Kв‰¤1051 \le N, M, K \le 10^51в‰¤N,M,Kв‰¤105).

The next KKK lines describe deliveries. Each line contains 4 space-separated integers xix_ixiвЂ‹, yiy_iyiвЂ‹, xiвЂІx'_ixiвЂІвЂ‹, yiвЂІy'_iyiвЂІвЂ‹ (1в‰¤xi,xiвЂІв‰¤N1 \le x_i, x'_i \le N1в‰¤xiвЂ‹,xiвЂІвЂ‹в‰¤N, 1в‰¤yi,yiвЂІв‰¤M1 \le y_i, y'_i \le M1в‰¤yiвЂ‹,yiвЂІвЂ‹в‰¤M), describing the iii-th delivery.

###Output:
Print one integer -- the minimum sum of durations possible.

###Subtasks


Subtask 1 [17 points]: yi=yiв€’1y_i = y_{i-1}yiвЂ‹=yiв€’1вЂ‹ for all i>0i>0i>0.
Subtask 2 [34 points]: Kв‰¤10K \le 10Kв‰¤10.
Subtask 3 [49 points]: No additional constraints.
###Sample Input 1:
5 4 3
2 3 4 1
1 4 5 2
3 3 5 3

###Sample Output 1:
16

###Explanation:

![fig2](https://codechef_shared.s3.amazonaws.com/download/HYC/External_contest_images/UWCOI21/sample1.png =600x400)

Salmon can turn the 3rd horizontal road into a highway.


For the first delivery, we can go 2 units to the right taking 2 units of time, and 2 units down taking 4 units of time. The time taken is 2+4=62 + 4 = 62+4=6.
For the second delivery, we can go 1 unit down taking 2 units of time, 4 units right taking 4 units of time, and 1 unit down taking 2 units of time. The time taken is 2+4+2=82 + 4 + 2 = 82+4+2=8.
For the third delivery, we can go 2 units right taking 2 units of time.
Therefore, the total time taken is 6+8+2=166 + 8 + 2 = 166+8+2=16.

###Sample Input 2:
10 2 3
1 2 10 2
2 1 9 1
4 1 7 1

###Sample Output 2:
23

###Explanation:
![fig1](https://codechef_shared.s3.amazonaws.com/download/HYC/External_contest_images/UWCOI21/sample2.png =600x300)

Salmon can turn the 1st horizontal road into a highway.


For the first delivery, we can go 1 unit down taking 2 units of time, 9 units right taking 9 units of time, and 1 unit up taking 2 units of time. The time taken is 2+9+2=132 + 9 + 2 = 132+9+2=13.
For the second delivery, we can go 7 units right taking 7 units of time.
For the third delivery, we can go 3 units to the right taking 3 units of time.
Therefore, the total time taken is 13+7+3=2313 + 7 + 3 = 2313+7+3=23.
